stories.headline: Researchers develop a fast locality simulator for gemm design-space exploration on multi-chiplet gpus
synopsis: A new simulator has been developed to improve the design of multi-chiplet GPUs, with the potential to increase compute throughput by up to 20%.
The simulator was created by a team of researchers who specialize in GPU architecture and design.
The development of this simulator could lead to more efficient and powerful GPUs in the future.
stories.explanation: Think of it like a blueprint for building a more efficient house, but instead of a house, it's a GPU.

stories.headline: Making locality-aware gemm compatible with page-granularity placement on chiplet gpus
synopsis: A new technique has been developed to make locality-aware GEMM compatible with page-granularity placement on chiplet GPUs, which could improve performance by up to 15%.
The technique was developed by a team of researchers who specialize in GPU architecture and design.
The development of this technique could lead to more efficient and powerful GPUs in the future.
stories.explanation: It's like finding the most efficient way to pack a suitcase, but instead of clothes, it's data.

stories.headline: A fully gpu-based workflow for building physics emulators of hypersonic flows

The workflow was created by a team of researchers who specialize in computational fluid dynamics.
The development of this workflow could lead to more accurate and efficient simulations of hypersonic flows.
stories.explanation: It's like using a supercomputer to simulate the flow of air around a speeding bullet, but instead of a bullet, it's a spacecraft.
